title: Rꜣ nw prt m hrw (125)
titleEn: Book of the Dead of Ani — Spell 125: the Negative Confession
source: Papyrus of Ani (British Museum EA 10470), Book of the Dead Spell 125B — the 42 declarations of innocence addressed one by one to the assessor gods. Hieroglyphs transcribed from E. A. W. Budge, The Papyrus of Ani (1895), interlinear text, Plates XXXI-XXXII (pp. 198-203); Budge's transliteration and English gloss consulted as a meaning-check. Transliteration in the Leiden Unified convention; translation made fresh from the original grammar. Shown left-to-right; the papyrus runs right-to-left. Pronunciation convention: conventional Egyptological (langCode null, no TTS). Readable layer ("say"): a per-word vocalization index-aligned to the transliteration tokens, giving the conventional Egyptological reading (after Allen/Gardiner) — a neutral "e" is inserted between consonants; ꜣ, ꜥ → a; w → u/w; j, i → i; y → y; ṯ → tj; ḏ → dj; ḫ/ẖ → kh; š → sh; ḥ/h → h; q → q; z → s. Divine names take the standard modern form where a recognizable one exists (Bastet, Nefertem, Ptah, Atum, Min); otherwise names and toponyms are the rule-based vocalization of the Egyptian. This is an artificial teaching pronunciation (vowels are not reconstructed). It does double duty: the human-readable line shown via the reader's script toggle, and the input for the one-time synthesized audio. Budge's older vocalizations are not used in the reader (kept only as a meaning-check). No browser TTS (langCode null). — MIGRATED to the per-glyph interlinear model (2026-07-10): each sign carries its own Gardiner code, role (phonogram / phonetic complement / determinative / suffix) and transcription; words are atomic. Quadrat ('as written') groupings are PROVISIONAL standard blockings, not yet verified against Budge Plate XXXI. Reed M17 transcribed 'i'. A handful of names use defective, ideographic or graphically-interchanged writings, flagged honestly in the sentence notes.
about: Spell 125B, the famous 'Negative Confession': the dead person declares innocence before 42 assessor gods, one at a time — 'Hail, [god], who comes forth from [place]: I have not [sin].' All 42 declarations are here, each with hieroglyphs, transliteration, and translation. The hieroglyphs are transcribed from Budge's 1895 plates of the Papyrus of Ani; the transliteration follows the Leiden convention, and the translations are made fresh from the original. Shown left-to-right for reading; on the papyrus the text runs right-to-left.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓅱𓋴𓐍𓈖𓅓𓏏𓂻 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓉺𓊖 𓂜 𓁹𓀀 𓇋𓋴𓆑𓏏𓅪

morph.gloss: O!, hail (the vocative interjection that opens each declaration)
'Wide-strider', the 1st assessor god (Budge: Usekh-nemtet)
comes forth, 'who comes forth' (participle of pri, to go/come out)
in, from (preposition)
Heliopolis, the sun-cult city (Budge: Annu)
not; negates the verb ('I have not …')
to do, make (lemma iri)
I, me (1st-person singular suffix pronoun; the seated-man sign)
wrong, falsehood, disorder — the opposite of maꜥat

sentences.l: hail · Wide-strider · coming-forth · from · Heliopolis · not · do · I · wrong
sentences.i: Hail, Wide-strider, who comes forth from Heliopolis: I have not done wrong.
sentences.n: The template for every one of the 42 declarations: 'Hail, [god], who comes forth from [place]: I have not [sin].' After a few you will read straight past the frame and just watch the god, the place, and the sin change. wsḫ-nmtt = 'wide of stride'; iwnw is Heliopolis (Budge's 'Annu'); isft, 'wrong / falsehood', is the opposite of maꜥat.

morph.gloss: O!, hail (the vocative interjection that opens each declaration)
'Embracer of flame', the 2nd assessor god (Budge: Hept-shet)
comes forth, 'who comes forth' (participle of pri, to go/come out)
in, from (preposition)
Kher-aha, a district on the Nile near Heliopolis (Old Cairo)
not; negates the verb ('I have not …')
to plunder, rob
I, me (1st-person singular suffix pronoun; the seated-man sign)

sentences.l: hail · Embracer-of-flame · coming-forth · from · Kher-aha · not · plunder · I
sentences.i: Hail, Embracer-of-flame, who comes forth from Kher-aha: I have not plundered.
sentences.n: ḥpt-sšt = 'embracer of flame' (Budge: 'Hept, embraced with flame'). ḫr-ꜥḥꜣ, Kher-aha, is an old district by the Nile near Heliopolis (Old Cairo). ꜥwꜣ, 'plunder, rob', is the very verb from the first draft — here in its real setting.

sentences.l: hail · Fenti · coming-forth · from · Hermopolis · not · steal · I
sentences.i: Hail, Fenti, who comes forth from Hermopolis: I have not stolen.
sentences.n: fnṯw, 'the Nose' (Budge 'Fenti'), a god named for the nose. ḫmnw is Hermopolis, the 'city of the Eight' primeval gods. ṯꜣw = 'steal, take away'.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓂝𓅓𓈙𓅱𓏏 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓈎𓂋𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓅓𓄿𓌪𓀀 𓂋𓍿𓀀𓁐𓏥

morph.gloss: O!, hail (the vocative interjection that opens each declaration)
'Eater of shades', the 4th assessor god; glyphs write šwt 'shadow' (Budge romanizes Am-khaibitu)
comes forth, 'who comes forth' (participle of pri, to go/come out)
in, from (preposition)
Qernet, the place the 4th god comes forth from
not; negates the verb ('I have not …')
to slay, kill
I, me (1st-person singular suffix pronoun; the seated-man sign)
people, human beings, mankind

sentences.l: hail · Eater-of-shades · coming-forth · from · Qernet · not · slay · I · men
sentences.i: Hail, Eater-of-shades, who comes forth from Qernet: I have not slain men.
sentences.n: ꜥm-šwt = 'eater of shades' (written ꜥ-m-š-w-t, with šwt 'shadow'; Budge: Am-khaibitu) — one of the more sinister assessors. smꜣ = 'slay, kill'; rmṯ = 'people, mankind'. Budge marks the men-sign 'twice', i.e. written to mean people in general.

sentences.l: hail · Dangerous-face · coming-forth · from · Rosetau · not · defraud · I · offerings
sentences.i: Hail, Dangerous-face, who comes forth from Rosetau: I have not defrauded the offerings.
sentences.n: nḥꜣ-ḥr = 'fierce / dangerous of face' (Budge 'Neha-hra'). rꜣ-sṯꜣw, Rosetau, is the necropolis realm of Sokar near Giza. ḥḏ = 'damage, defraud'; dbḥ, the offerings that are due.

sentences.l: hail · Double-Lion · coming-forth · from · sky · not · diminish · I
sentences.i: Hail, Double-Lion, who comes forth from heaven: I have not diminished the oblations.
sentences.n: rwti (Budge 'Rereti'), the 'Double Lion' — the twin lions Shu and Tefnut, guardians of the horizon; here the god comes forth from the sky (pt). ḫbꜣ = 'diminish, reduce'. Budge restores the object '[oblations]' from parallel copies — the sin is stinting the gods' food-offerings.

sentences.l: hail · Blood-eater · coming-forth · from · the-block · not · commit · I · deceit
sentences.i: Hail, Blood-eater, who comes forth from the block: I have not acted deceitfully.
sentences.n: ꜥm-snf — 'Eater of blood' (Budge: Am-snef); comes forth from nmmt, 'the block / place of slaughter'. The sin: I have not acted deceitfully. The sin-word is written ḥ-n-w-t; its exact reading is uncertain (given here as ḥnwt), but the sense — deceit / crookedness — is clear.

sentences.n: wꜣmmti — 'Uamemti'; from the place of execution. The sin: I have not lain with another man's wife. As in declaration 20, the first sign of ḥmt 'wife' (𓍔) is an unexpected transcription for the ḥm-sign — flagged as uncertain.

glossary.i: i — O!, hail (the vocative interjection that opens each declaration)
glossary.pr: pr — comes forth, 'who comes forth' (participle of pri, to go/come out)
glossary.iwnw: iwnw — Heliopolis, the sun-cult city (Budge: Annu)
glossary.n: n — not; negates the verb ('I have not …')
glossary.·i: ·i — I, me (1st-person singular suffix pronoun; the seated-man sign)
glossary.isft: isft — wrong, falsehood, disorder — the opposite of maꜥat
glossary.ḥpt-sšt: ḥpt-sšt — 'Embracer of flame', the 2nd assessor god (Budge: Hept-shet)
glossary.ḫr-ꜥḥꜣ: ḫr-ꜥḥꜣ — Kher-aha, a district on the Nile near Heliopolis (Old Cairo)
glossary.fnṯw: fnṯw — 'the Nose', the 3rd assessor god (Budge: Fenti)
glossary.ḫmnw: ḫmnw — Hermopolis, city of the eight primeval gods (Budge: Khemennu)
glossary.qrt: qrt — Qernet, the place the 4th god comes forth from
glossary.nḥꜣ-ḥr: nḥꜣ-ḥr — 'Dangerous of face', the 5th assessor god (Budge: Neha-hra)
glossary.rꜣ-sṯꜣw: rꜣ-sṯꜣw — Rosetau, the Sokar necropolis realm near Giza
glossary.rwti: rwti — the 'Double Lion' (Shu and Tefnut), the 6th assessor god (Budge: Rereti)
glossary.mꜣꜣ-f: mꜣꜣ-f — 'Whose eyes are of fire', 7th assessor (Budge: Fenti-of-fire)
glossary.ḫt-nṯr: ḫt-nṯr — the property of the god
glossary.grg: grg — to speak falsehood, tell lies
glossary.nḥm: nḥm — to take away, carry off, snatch
glossary.ḥwt-kꜣ-ptḥ: ḥwt-kꜣ-ptḥ — Memphis ('Mansion of the ka of Ptah'; Budge: Het-ka-Ptah)
glossary.kn: kn — to be violent, do harm
glossary.imntt: imntt — the West, the realm of the dead
glossary.nk: nk — to copulate; (here) fornicate / commit adultery
glossary.srmi: srmi — to make (someone) weep (causative of rmi)
glossary.wš: wš — to lay waste, make desolate
glossary.mꜣꜥti: mꜣꜥti — Maati, the hall/city of the Two Truths
glossary.ṯnmiw: ṯnmiw — 'He who strides backward', 18th assessor (Budge: Tenemiu)
glossary.šm-r: šm-r — 'to set the mouth going' — to speak against, babble
glossary.mꜣꜣ-int-f: mꜣꜣ-int-f — 'Watcher of what is brought to him', the 22nd assessor god (Budge: Maa-antu-f)
glossary.wsṯn: wsṯn — to act/judge with undue haste
glossary.sꜣwti: sꜣwti — Sais (a writing of the town; cf. sꜣw; Budge: Sauti)
glossary.sqꜣ: sqꜣ — to make high/loud (causative of qꜣi)
glossary.wḏ-rḫyt: wḏ-rḫyt — 'Commander of mankind', the 37th assessor god (Budge: Uatch-rekhit)
glossary.pꜣwt-nṯrw: pꜣwt-nṯrw — the cake-offerings of the gods
glossary.ꜥqw-nḫn: ꜥqw-nḫn — the food of a child
glossary.nṯr-niwt-f: nṯr-niwt-f — the god of his town
glossary.tꜣ-rd: tꜣ-rd — 'Fiery of legs' (Budge: 'Blazing legs'), the 14th assessor god
glossary.ꜣḫḫ: ꜣḫḫ — 'the darkness' (Budge: Akhekh), the place the 14th god comes forth from
glossary.ꜥm-snf: ꜥm-snf — 'Eater of blood', the 15th assessor god (Budge: Am-snef)
glossary.nmmt: nmmt — 'the block, the place of slaughter/execution'
glossary.ḥr-wr: ḥr-wr — 'Chief of the mighty' (Budge: Her-uru), the 23rd assessor god
glossary.ꜣmmt: ꜣmmt — Amemt, the place the 23rd god comes forth from
glossary.wtn: wtn — Uten, the place the 31st god comes forth from
glossary.nb-ꜥbwi: nb-ꜥbwi — 'Lord of two horns', the 32nd assessor god (Budge: Neb-abui)
glossary.ꜥš-ḫrw: ꜥš-ḫrw — 'loud of voice'; to multiply one's words, talk overmuch
glossary.sꜣq: sꜣq — robbery, theft (Budge reads the damaged sin here with a query)
glossary.ꜥm-šwt: ꜥm-šwt — 'Eater of shades', the 4th assessor god; glyphs write šwt 'shadow' (Budge romanizes Am-khaibitu)
glossary.ḥnwt: ḥnwt — deceit, wrongdoing (exact reading uncertain; the sin is acting deceitfully)
glossary.twtw-f: twtw-f — 'Doubly wicked', the 20th assessor (Budge: Tutu-f); written t-w-t-w-f
